title: The One Amigo

jokes.text: Step away from the car and no one gets hurt! Ow! To the employment cave!
jokes.setup: Bender addressing his car like a hostage situation
jokes.punchline: The over-the-top dramatic response followed by calling Planet Express 'the employment cave'
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender absurdity treating mundane situations dramatically, with good character voice

jokes.text: Whoa, whoa, there, girl. Go on now! Get on back to Paraguay!
jokes.setup: Character riding what sounds like a horse to work
jokes.punchline: Dismissing the 'horse' back to Paraguay of all places
jokes.explanation: Unexpected specificity of Paraguay makes this land well, good absurdist world-building

jokes.text: We're being eaten by a giant spider! There's no time for that! The Professor needs us!
jokes.setup: Someone panicking about being eaten by a giant spider
jokes.punchline: Dismissing mortal peril because the boss called a meeting
jokes.explanation: Great workplace comedy - prioritizing mundane over life-threatening with perfect deadpan delivery

jokes.text: What is it?! I'm on the edge of my butt!
jokes.setup: Professor building up anticipation for big announcement
jokes.punchline: Fry's malapropism of 'edge of my seat' to 'edge of my butt'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry stupidity - the kind of malapropism that's both dumb and endearing

jokes.text: soda machine! (all cheering) You undersold it, Professor!
jokes.setup: Professor hyping up 'greatest announcement in Planet Express history'
jokes.punchline: It's just a soda machine, but Bender acts like it exceeded expectations
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect character follow-up - the anticlimax becomes a win

jokes.text: Look, it has Slurm Loco! It's the extremiest!
jokes.setup: Fry excited about soda options
jokes.punchline: Made-up superlative 'extremiest' for energy drink parody
jokes.explanation: Basic brand parody with made-up word. Functional but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: Hey, what can I get y'all?
jokes.setup: Introduction of new soda machine character
jokes.punchline: The machine has a Southern accent and personality
jokes.explanation: Standard Futurama anthropomorphizing of machines, sets up character but not particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: Y'all can't drink at work! This ain't a sawmill!
jokes.setup: Bender asking for alcoholic sodas
jokes.punchline: Machine's arbitrary distinction that sawmills allow workplace drinking
jokes.explanation: Great specificity - why a sawmill? Perfect absurd logic delivered with conviction

jokes.text: I call myself 'Bev.' And if you're looking for a smelly old can of booze, go look in the mirror.
jokes.setup: Bender insulting the beverage machine
jokes.punchline: Bev's comeback calling Bender a smelly old can of booze
jokes.explanation: Solid comeback that uses Bender's robot nature against him, establishes Bev's sassiness

jokes.text: And one more to wash that Slurm taste out of my mouth.
jokes.setup: Fry ordering another Slurm after just having one
jokes.punchline: Using more Slurm to wash out the Slurm taste
jokes.explanation: Perfect circular logic that captures both addiction and stupidity in one line

jokes.text: I was wondering who Shrekked in the toilet.
jokes.setup: Fry saying his pee was green from Slurm
jokes.punchline: Bender using 'Shrekked' as a verb for green bathroom activities
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference turned into crude humor, very quotable

jokes.text: Cut me some slack, preacher. I'm just a coal grinder's daughter trying to stay off the pole.
jokes.setup: Leela criticizing Bev for making Fry addicted
jokes.punchline: Bev's over-the-top sob story about being a coal grinder's daughter avoiding stripping
jokes.explanation: Perfect character establishment - trashy but endearing backstory delivered with conviction

jokes.text: With that big ol' caboose, it'd have to be a telephone pole!
jokes.setup: Bev's reference to staying off the pole
jokes.punchline: Bev's self-deprecating joke about needing a telephone pole due to her size
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor that builds on the previous line, shows Bev's wit

jokes.text: Well, I guess a fella's gotta talk big when he's sportin' a little shriveled-up antenna like that. What?! For your information, 'madam,' it's a grower, not a show-er!
jokes.setup: Bev insulting Bender's antenna size
jokes.punchline: Bender's defensive response about being a 'grower not a shower'
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual innuendo but works for robot anatomy, Bender's insecurity is funny

jokes.text: My antenna's fine. It's just fine. Ladies can't get enough Bender.
jokes.setup: Bender clearly bothered by Bev's insult
jokes.punchline: Overly defensive reassurance to himself that sounds pathetic
jokes.explanation: Perfect insecurity response from Bender - the repetition and self-reassurance rings true

jokes.text: Call me old-fashioned, but I like a little romance in an orgy.
jokes.setup: Bender about to get intimate with Bev and other robots
jokes.punchline: Wanting romance in an orgy as being 'old-fashioned'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contradiction - treating romantic sentiment as conservative while planning an orgy. Perfect economy and unexpectedness

jokes.text: Just sitting here turning quarters into urine.
jokes.setup: Bender asking what Fry is doing at 2am
jokes.punchline: Fry's matter-of-fact description of the economics of drinking
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical observation that reframes drinking in transactional terms, very quotable

jokes.text: We don't gotta put up with this. We got Poli Sci degrees.
jokes.setup: Robot prostitutes being insulted by Fry's presence
jokes.punchline: Their dignity comes from having Political Science degrees
jokes.explanation: Great satire of both prostitution and liberal arts education - the specificity of Poli Sci makes it land

jokes.text: Hot. I could bake a potato in my cleavage.
jokes.setup: Robot saying she's hot
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation with specific food item
jokes.explanation: Solid robot humor - taking metaphor literally with specific, absurd detail

jokes.text: I ain't makin' drinks for no trashy robo-sluts.
jokes.setup: Bender asking for drinks for the prostitutes
jokes.punchline: The slutty soda machine calling others trashy sluts
jokes.explanation: Good hypocrisy humor - Bev judging others while being equally promiscuous

jokes.text: Trashy? At least I don't put out for quarters.
jokes.setup: Bev calling the robots trashy
jokes.punchline: Robot pointing out Bev literally puts out (drinks) for quarters
jokes.explanation: Clever comeback that uses the literal mechanics of vending machines as sexual innuendo

jokes.text: All right, I've been waiting all day for an excuse to hit a lady.
jokes.setup: Fight brewing between Bender and Bev
jokes.punchline: Bender's enthusiasm about having an excuse for domestic violence
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender awfulness - the honesty about wanting to hit women is horrible but in character

jokes.text: You try it, and I'll bash your face in. Ooh, I'm so scared, No-Arms.
jokes.setup: Bev threatening Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender mocking her threat by pointing out she has no arms
jokes.explanation: Good logical comeback that highlights the absurdity of a vending machine fighting

jokes.text: You're glowing like the Human Torch on prom night.
jokes.setup: Fry glowing from radiation
jokes.punchline: Specific pop culture reference with sexual implication
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference but relies on recognition rather than cleverness

jokes.text: Shouldn't you be standing on a rocky coast somewhere preventing shipwrecks?
jokes.setup: Fry glowing brightly
jokes.punchline: Reference to lighthouse imagery
jokes.explanation: Good visual metaphor that extends the glowing joke logically

jokes.text: Nice, hate me because of the brightness of my skin.
jokes.setup: Leela making jokes about Fry's glowing
jokes.punchline: Fry treating it like racial discrimination
jokes.explanation: Clever social commentary that reframes his radiation as a race issue, well-constructed

jokes.text: You okay, ma'am? I think your soda water broke.
jokes.setup: Bev making groaning sounds during what turns out to be labor
jokes.punchline: Fry's pun on 'water breaking' with soda machine
jokes.explanation: Excellent pun that works on multiple levels - pregnancy and soda machine mechanics

jokes.text: My God! Bev just had a baby in my cup!
jokes.setup: Bev giving birth as a vending machine
jokes.punchline: The baby robot literally being dispensed into Fry's cup
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly gross visual that perfectly combines vending machine mechanics with birth

jokes.text: Aw, he's so cute. Wait. No, he isn't.
jokes.setup: Looking at newborn robot baby
jokes.punchline: Immediate correction after actually seeing the baby
jokes.explanation: Good timing joke that subverts expected baby cooing with honest assessment

jokes.text: Wipe my tiny metal ass!
jokes.setup: Baby robot's first words
jokes.punchline: Crude demand that sounds like both baby talk and Bender
jokes.explanation: Perfect establishing moment showing the baby inherited Bender's crudeness

jokes.text: Ugh! In public?
jokes.setup: Bev breastfeeding (dispensing milk) to her baby
jokes.punchline: Amy's prudish reaction to public breastfeeding from a vending machine
jokes.explanation: Good social commentary on public breastfeeding controversy applied to absurd robot situation

jokes.text: Are you telling me the stuff we did in private, and also twice on the sidewalk, made a baby robot? How?!
jokes.setup: Bender confused about robot reproduction
jokes.punchline: Admitting to public sex while still claiming ignorance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - being crude about details while missing the obvious connection

jokes.text: No. She was a religious fundamentalist. Plus she didn't have a mouth. It's an unusual combination.
jokes.setup: Asked if his mom told him about robot reproduction
jokes.punchline: Mom was fundamentalist AND had no mouth - treating both as equal obstacles
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation where the physical impossibility is treated as equally problematic to the religious views

jokes.text: No one here will laugh at you for not knowing where robot babies come from. (all laughing) We don't know either.
jokes.setup: Leela reassuring Bender no one will laugh
jokes.punchline: Everyone immediately laughs, then admits they don't know either
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that sets up everyone as equally ignorant, nice timing

jokes.text: Hey, he looks like me! His hideous appearance is a by-product...
jokes.setup: Bender excited to see a robot that looks like him
jokes.punchline: Narrator immediately calling the robot hideously ugly
jokes.explanation: Good cruel juxtaposition - Bender's pride immediately undercut by objective assessment

jokes.text: After beer and hot wings at a local gas station, the two enjoy intimate time behind a Dumpster.
jokes.setup: Educational film about robot courtship
jokes.punchline: Describes extremely unromantic date scenario in clinical educational terms
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between educational film tone and trashy robot romance scenario. Good economy of language.

jokes.text: The best he can do is Francine. (buzzer sounds)
jokes.setup: Gerald looking for a female robot
jokes.punchline: Francine is so unattractive she gets a game show buzzer
jokes.explanation: Mean-spirited but the buzzer sound effect gives it some punch

jokes.text: Let's watch, aroused, as Gerald's antenna uploads a binary file to Francine's internal drive.
jokes.setup: Educational film explaining robot reproduction
jokes.punchline: Narrator encouraging sexual arousal at technical computer process
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody of educational films with the inappropriate 'aroused' making it creepy and funny

jokes.text: Except masturbation. That's just wrong.
jokes.setup: Everything your body does is perfectly natural
jokes.punchline: Arbitrary moral exception for masturbation
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of sex education hypocrisy with great punchline timing

jokes.text: Relax, Bender. No one would let you near a child. Bev will get custody, and you'll be a deadbeat Dad who never even bothers to know his own son.
jokes.setup: Bender panicking about not being ready for fatherhood
jokes.punchline: Leela reassuring him by saying he'll be a terrible absent father
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfect character moment - Leela's 'comfort' is brutally honest assessment

jokes.text: (sniffles): You really think so? I know so. You're absolutely horrible in every way. You're sweet, Leela.
jokes.setup: Leela calling Bender horrible in every way
jokes.punchline: Bender taking this as a compliment and calling her sweet
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - completely missing insults and finding comfort in cruelty

jokes.text: So here's my certificate of abandonment drawn up by our notary! That'll be ten bucks.
jokes.setup: Bender trying to abandon his child
jokes.punchline: Hermes charging for notarizing child abandonment
jokes.explanation: Great bureaucratic absurdity - treating child abandonment as routine paperwork with fees

jokes.text: Damn baby's drivin' me crazy! He won't stop giggling no matter how much I scream at him!
jokes.setup: Bev complaining about the baby
jokes.punchline: Her solution to baby giggles is screaming, which makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Good dark parenting humor showing Bev's complete lack of maternal instincts

jokes.text: I still got womanly needs! Who's gonna look twice at me with this dirty leech hangin' off my dairy nozzle?
jokes.setup: Bev explaining why she can't keep the baby
jokes.punchline: Calling the baby a 'dirty leech' on her 'dairy nozzle'
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly crude and specific language that combines vending machine terminology with maternal complaints

jokes.text: I left my window open, and moths got in. I swear, if I wasn't such a great Dad, I'd grab his ugly little neck and...
jokes.setup: Baby Ben can't sleep, Bender frustrated
jokes.punchline: Bender's violent impulse interrupted by self-praise as a father
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark Bender humor - violent thoughts immediately contradicted by delusional self-image

jokes.text: Ben'! Ben'! He loves bending, just like me. Aw, I'm gonna call him Ben... after the first half of me... Bender.
jokes.setup: Baby enjoys watching Bender bend things
jokes.punchline: Bender's narcissistic naming logic treating 'Ben' as half of 'Bender'
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Bender's egotism in naming, sweet but selfish

jokes.text: Bend it like it called you poo-poo-face! It called me what?!
jokes.setup: Ben encouraging Bender to bend something
jokes.punchline: Bender getting genuinely offended by the imaginary insult
jokes.explanation: Sweet father-son moment with Bender's childish offense at imaginary name-calling

jokes.text: - Leela was wrong! - Leela was wrong!
jokes.setup: Bender proving he can be a good parent
jokes.punchline: Teaching his son to chant insults about Leela
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Bender's petty victory, teaching kid bad habits while bonding

jokes.text: But my mommy had no arms! Does that mean I can never be a bender?
jokes.setup: Learning that bending ability comes from mothers
jokes.punchline: Ben's logical concern about inheriting from armless mother
jokes.explanation: Good logical extension of the established world rules, sets up central conflict

jokes.text: I hate to crush a boy's dreams, but what the heck.
jokes.setup: Professor about to explain Ben can't bend
jokes.punchline: Professor's casual cruelty disguised as reluctance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor moment - fake reluctance followed by enthusiastic cruelty

jokes.text: Don't tell my son what he can and can't do! You may know what's in his head, but you don't know what's in his heart! - There's no slot in there either. - I said shut up!
jokes.setup: Bender's passionate defense of his son's potential
jokes.punchline: Professor's technical correction about heart slots, Bender's frustrated response
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of inspirational father speech with technical robot reality

jokes.text: I'd like to thank the Preacherbot for the introduction and also for not circumcising me again, am I right? Twice was enough.
jokes.setup: Ben giving speech at his bar mitzvah equivalent
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to being circumcised twice as a robot
jokes.explanation: Disturbing and funny - what would robot circumcision even mean? Great dark humor

jokes.text: (sniffs) I could take him or leave him.
jokes.setup: Asked if he's proud of his son after emotional speech
jokes.punchline: Bender's emotional denial while clearly moved to tears
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender emotional moment - unable to admit feelings while clearly having them

jokes.text: I'm back for my son! I've come to take him and leave with him! She beat you to both things.
jokes.setup: Bev dramatically announcing she's taking her son and leaving
jokes.punchline: Hermes pointing out the redundancy in her statement
jokes.explanation: Good Hermes pedantry moment, deflating dramatic declaration with grammar correction

jokes.text: Well, I'm still less unfitter than you. - Says who?! - Says this document you gave me when you tried to abandon Ben first!
jokes.setup: Bev and Bender arguing about who's less fit as parent
jokes.punchline: Bev using Bender's own abandonment certificate against him
jokes.explanation: Good plot callback that creates dramatic irony - Bender's selfishness used against him

jokes.text: That doesn't prove I'm an unfit parent! I was drunk and violent when I signed that!
jokes.setup: Bender defending his fitness as parent
jokes.punchline: His defense actually makes him sound worse as a parent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - completely undermining his own argument while trying to defend himself

jokes.text: For a guy who's not too bright, you're too damn bright! Now, get out! And don't come back until someone finds a use for a million-watt idiot!
jokes.setup: Bender upset about losing his son, Fry's radioactivity bothering him
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'bright' meaning both intelligent and literally glowing
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that sets up Fry's role in the climax while showing Bender's pain

jokes.text: That's it, Fry! For a guy who's not too bright, you're too damn bright! Now, get out! And don't come back until someone finds a use for a million-watt idiot!
jokes.setup: Fry's glowing accidentally destroys something important to Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender plays on 'bright' meaning both intelligent and luminous
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay connecting Fry's stupidity with his literal brightness. Great setup and payoff structure.

jokes.text: Mom, will I ever get to see Dad again? No, he died yesterday. Rust monsters ate his face. Sweet dreams!
jokes.setup: Ben asking innocent question about seeing his father
jokes.punchline: Bev's cruel lie about Bender's death in graphic detail, then cheerful goodnight
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - the casual cruelty followed by cheerful 'sweet dreams' is perfectly awful

jokes.text: You're under arrest for kidnapping and burning raccoons without a permit!
jokes.setup: Police arresting Bender for kidnapping his own son
jokes.punchline: Additional charge for burning raccoons without proper permits
jokes.explanation: Good absurd bureaucracy humor - treating wildlife burning as permit issue rather than cruelty

jokes.text: - There's a dam! - Damn! - There's a grate! - Great!
jokes.setup: Father and son running from police
jokes.punchline: Each obstacle's name sounds like the other's reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay but works in the moment, shows father-son connection through shared responses

jokes.text: Yeah, this ain't gonna happen. Is that even necessary?
jokes.setup: Ben straining dramatically to try to bend the grate
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of failure and questioning the dramatic pose
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of dramatic moment with casual realism and self-aware humor

jokes.text: I'm amazed someone who dispenses such warm soda, could be so coldhearted!
jokes.setup: Leela criticizing Bev's parenting
jokes.punchline: Playing on temperature metaphors - warm soda vs cold heart
jokes.explanation: Decent pun connecting Bev's function as soda machine to her personality flaws

jokes.text: I'm gonna buy a drink just so I can throw it in your face!
jokes.setup: Leela angry at Bev
jokes.punchline: Plans to support Bev's business purely to enable face-throwing gesture
jokes.explanation: Great spite logic - willing to give money to someone you hate for gesture purposes. Good escalation.

jokes.text: You got knocked up by a police officer while he was looking for your kidnapped child? Sure did! I still got it.
jokes.setup: Discovering Bev had another baby during the police chase
jokes.punchline: Bev treating the inappropriate timing as proof of her attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bev moment - completely inappropriate behavior treated as personal victory

jokes.text: Damn it, I love that little guy so much! I'd sacrifice any four of you if it would help him bend even a little!
jokes.setup: Bender expressing love for his son
jokes.punchline: Willing to sacrifice specific number of friends for minor improvement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender love - genuinely touching but characteristically horrible. The specificity of 'four' makes it brilliant

jokes.text: He'd be able to bend, but he'd lose all his memories. He wouldn't even remember who you are.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining the surgery option
jokes.punchline: The horrible trade-off that creates the episode's central dilemma
jokes.explanation: Not funny per se, but creates the emotional stakes that make the ending powerful

jokes.text: Dad, are you crying? No! I said with the mouth you're looking at.
jokes.setup: Ben noticing Bender is emotional about the memory loss
jokes.punchline: Bender's ridiculous denial referencing his mouth while clearly crying from his eyes
jokes.explanation: Good Bender emotional denial with absurd logic about which facial feature is 'talking'

jokes.text: I never knew my father. It's not important.
jokes.setup: Ben after losing his memories, not recognizing Bender
jokes.punchline: Casually dismissing the importance of fathers while his heartbroken dad stands there
jokes.explanation: Devastating dramatic irony - the casual dismissal while Bender suffers in silence is brilliantly cruel

jokes.text: It's just not possible to get you to bending school this year. Or is there?
jokes.setup: Bender giving up on getting Ben to school through the fog
jokes.punchline: Fry's dramatic entrance as human lighthouse
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to Bender's earlier insult - Fry's stupidity and radioactivity save the day

tmdb_overview: After Bender sells an NFT representing the "Concept of Bender", he feels lost and returns to his ancestral village in Mexico to rediscover himself.
